sections.paragraph:
i.q. דּוּר to go in a circle, especially quickly (comp. also דָּרַר). Hence-

(1) to be borne on swiftly, to press on swiftly, used of a horse and rider, Nah 3:2, pr. to go in a circle, as is the custom of those who break in or exercise horses. See the noun דַּהֲרָה .

(2) [“to go in a circle, hence”] to endure long. Hence תִּדְהָר, and-
